article_meta.title: Business Climate change study that overestimated economic impact is pulled — but not before banks relied on it By Taylor Herzlich Published Dec

reasoning: Mostly neutral summary but includes several loaded descriptors (e.g., “staggering,” “dramatically overstate,” “exaggerated”) that mirror or amplify the article’s tone. Factual content aligns with the source and includes opposing context that risks remain.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses interpretive wording like “dramatically overstate” and “exaggerated estimate.”
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: All main claims trace to the article; one minor overreach calling NGFS use “policymaking.”
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Labels the 62% figure as “staggering,” presenting a value judgment as fact.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: A few elevated terms heighten drama but do not dominate the summary.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No claims about intentions or beliefs.
custom_reasoning.slant: Includes both the retraction and the authors’ view that significant risks remain.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Omits attribution to the separate economists identifying the Uzbekistan issue and NGFS’s update timeline statement.
custom_reasoning.word_choice: No politically charged terms; mostly neutral vocabulary.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Balances error/retraction with context that risks remain.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Generally neutral but retains some of the source’s heightened tone.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Captures authors’ and institutional use; misses distinct critic economists’ perspective and NGFS’s stated plan details.
